svg.text: Deterministic formula for maximum fusion depth based on cache hierarchy

g.text: Weights: 3 × H × H × 2B = 24MB → MUST stream from DRAM
Each head: Q_h[1×64], K_h[1×64], V_h[1×64] = 128B each = 2 cache lines!
Output per head: O_h[1 × 64] = 128B = 2 cache lines (stays in registers!)

svg.text: T = KV tile size for flash attn
B = bytes per element (2 for FP16)
Constraint 1: Per-Head Flash Attention fits in L1
Q_h + K_tile + V_tile + O_accum + softmax_stats ≤ S_L1
Constraint 2: Solve for max KV tile size T
Total: 16.8KB per head ✓ fits L1 with room for prefetch!
→ d=64, B=2: 128B = 2 cache lines per head ✓
Each head writes to separate cache lines → no false sharing!

g.text: Level 1: Per-Head Fusion (fits in L1 + Registers)
DRAM traffic per head: 0 (Q,K,V already in cache from QKV proj)
L2 traffic: stream KV cache tiles → L1
Output: O_h written to L2 buffer (128B) only when complete
Can run n_h heads in parallel on different cores!

svg.text: KEY INSIGHT: Fuse per-head completely (RoPE→Flash→Output), parallelize across heads
Each head is independent until output projection. Each head's working set fits in L1. Zero inter-core communication until final sync!
4. DRAM Traffic: What MUST Touch Memory

g.text: Past context, tile T at a time

svg.text: T_kv = floor((S_L1 × 0.8 - 2×d×B) / (2×d×B)) // KV tile for flash attention
T_kv = min(T_kv, 64) // Cap at cache line multiple
require: d × B ≥ cache_line // Each head on own cache line
✓ All heads' outputs fit L2 before output projection
✓ Head boundaries align to cache lines
✓ RoPE cos/sin for position fits L1
// Step 2: QKV projection (stream weights, output to L2)
gemm_qkv_fused(Q, K, V, x, W_qkv, H);
// Per-head flash attention (ALL in L1 + registers)
// Load KV tile to L1 (prefetch next)
